qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: GraphQL public introspection guard bypass exposes schema types and Cloud Code function names
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory is a Postgres-only SQL injection in `src/Adapters/Storage/Postgres/PostgresStorageAdapter.js` during dot-notation `Increment` handling. This finding is a GraphQL introspection disclosure in `src/GraphQL/ParseGraphQLServer.js` and does not reach the PostgreSQL adapter or any SQL-construction sink.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Verification email request endpoint discloses account existence and verification state
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory root cause is unsafe interpolation of dot-notation `Increment` sub-key names into PostgreSQL update SQL in `PostgresStorageAdapter`. This finding is a response-differentiation bug in `src/Routers/UsersRouter.js` for `POST /verificationEmailRequest` and does not involve nested-object updates, `Increment`, or Postgres SQL generation.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described root cause: attacker-controlled dot-notation sub-key names breaking out of PostgreSQL string literals during `Increment` handling in `src/Adapters/Storage/Postgres/PostgresStorageAdapter.js`.
evidence: The advisory fix commit `aa0de68d` between `8.6.30` and `8.6.31` changes only `src/Adapters/Storage/Postgres/PostgresStorageAdapter.js`, escaping single quotes before interpolating nested `Increment` sub-key names into the `CONCAT('{"...":', COALESCE($...->>'...','0')::int + ...)::jsonb` SQL fragment.

The `graphql_introspection_type_bypass` finding targets `POST /graphql` and `src/GraphQL/ParseGraphQLServer.js`, where a raw `__schema` substring check fails to block `__type` introspection. It is a schema-metadata disclosure bug and does not touch Postgres storage code or SQL update construction.

The `verification_email_request_account_enumeration` finding targets `POST /verificationEmailRequest` and `src/Routers/UsersRouter.js`, where branch-specific responses after a `_User` lookup disclose account state. It does not involve the Postgres adapter, dot-notation field updates, or SQL injection mechanics.
